title: Gemma Playground: Robot Duck
summary: Xavier Plantaz showcases Open Duck Mini v2 robots running Gemma 4 E2B on Raspberry Pi 5 and Jetson Orin Nano. The setup combines Parakeet for speech-to-text, Gemma 4 for inference, and Kokoro for text-to-speech to enable real-time multimodal interaction on-device.
writeup: A live demo of two Open Duck Mini v2 bipedal robot ducks running Gemma 4 2B entirely on-device: one on a Raspberry Pi 5 via LiteRT, one on a Jetson Orin Nano. The ducks use Parakeet for speech-to-text, Gemma 4 for inference, and Cocoro for text-to-speech, processing multimodal input from built-in microphones and cameras. The demo shows real-time Q&A and self-introduction with low latency, establishing that a sub-2B model is sufficient for responsive conversational robotics at the edge. The presenter names autonomous locomotion and multi-duck peer interaction as the immediate next steps.

key_points: Gemma 4 2B runs fully on-device on a Raspberry Pi 5 using the LiteRT runtime, demonstrating that edge inference for conversational robotics no longer requires cloud offload.
The full audio pipeline combines three open-source components in sequence: Parakeet handles speech-to-text, Gemma 4 handles language inference, and Cocoro handles text-to-speech output.

The Jetson Orin Nano serves as the second hardware target alongside the Raspberry Pi 5, showing the same Gemma 4 2B model deploys across both ARM-class edge boards with camera and microphone peripherals.
The Open Duck Mini v2 project (by Antoine Piron on GitHub) adds physical expressiveness through antenna motion and LED states tied to attention and boot phases, not just verbal output.
The presenter identifies the next capability milestone as autonomous walking and multi-agent peer interaction, where the ducks would see each other and communicate without human prompting.

chapters.title: Next Steps: Walking and Peer Interaction
